mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or unemployment spikes fail to predict negative returns with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic momentum, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales yields negative 60-day SPX returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity, triggering short-term risk-off positioning in equities.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns during tightening periods, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation remains below 2.5% while the federal funds rate stays under 1.0%, the Fed maintains accommodative policy, supporting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in >55% of months meeting the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day SPX returns, with rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining housing starts and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumer leverage and housing market stress, prompting rapid equity deleveraging over the next week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% MoM while RSAFS rises > 1% MoM
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive 5-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production alongside rising real GDP confirms synchronized economic recovery, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting equity markets over the next month.
failure_condition: Synchronized supply and demand expansion fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during recovery phases, with directional accuracy below 50%.
